_doc: The provider/framework matrix as DATA — the api-doc->http-mcp mapping, not code. A host runner expands x_axes x frameworks into wire calls: physics picks the tool (http_request | bidi_command), endpoint+caps_template build the target, inject_auth says where the resolved profile credential goes BELOW the boundary. http-mcp stays 3 tools; this is specs/ data, the same discipline as the route priors. Verified live on 2026-06-24 via http_request/bidi_command + auth profile prod:adminltqa.

auth.resolve: auth profile (env LT_USERNAME/LT_ACCESS_KEY or gitignored auth/<profile>.json) — the secret never crosses the agent boundary

inject_auth_shapes.basic_header: WebDriver/Appium/HyperExecute over HTTP — Authorization: Basic base64(user:key)
inject_auth_shapes.caps_url: CDP/Puppeteer ws — user/accessKey injected into LT:Options inside ?capabilities=<urlencoded json>

endpoints._framework_build_host_finding: VERIFIED 2026-06-24: the public api-doc curl says manual-api.lambdatest.com/framework/v1/espresso/build but that host returns 500 (nginx, cfOrigin) on the build route — manual-api is the UPLOAD host only. The live working build host is mobile-api.lambdatest.com/framework/v1/{espresso,xcui}/build (QA-sanctum prod). Auth (Basic) is accepted on both; only mobile-api routes the build. Run proof: POST mobile-api .../v1/espresso/build via http-mcp prod:adminltqa -> {status:[Success],buildId:[22983015]}, appeared Running on appautomation.lambdatest.com.

selenium-bidi.note: session created over HTTP (CALL); webSocketUrl:true returns a BiDi ws (CHANNEL) for bidi_command. se:cdp gives raw CDP on the same session.

appium.verified: wire-CALL-appium-vd-validate — apk_arena (com.example.apk_arena) on Galaxy S22 emulator, passed

puppeteer.note: raw CDP — bidi_command drives it; timeout_ms >= 60000 for cold-start.

playwright.note: CATCH: /playwright speaks Playwright's OWN protocol (a CDP superset), NOT raw CDP. bidi_command cannot drive it — a host-side Playwright client (8/pilot) must. For the raw-CDP path use the puppeteer entry.

cypress._doc: NOT HyperExecute, NOT a live wire. Cypress runs as a JOB: the lambdatest-cypress-cli zips the project + lambdatest-config.json and POSTs a build; results are pulled by GET. The public api-doc 'Cypress Automation > Cypress' section exposes only RESULT-fetch endpoints (captured 2026-06-24 over the channel) — the run is CLI-submitted, so this is a runner, not remote-control.

_smartui_build_status.note: visual-diff build status (NOT the espresso/xcui framework build status). Captured from public api-doc 2026-06-24.

espresso._doc: native Android instrumented suite. NOT an appium session — a framework BUILD: upload app+testSuite -> POST a build -> poll status. Endpoint mapped from public api-doc (channel, 2026-06-24); host corrected to mobile-api after manual-api 500'd; RUN-verified via http-mcp prod:adminltqa.
espresso.prereq_upload: POST framework_upload (multipart) appFile=<.apk>, type=espresso-android, name=<label> -> returns lt://APP... for both the app and the test suite

espresso-rd._doc: real-device cloud (api-doc: App Automation API REAL Devices). device names are physical models; the build host is mobile-api/framework/v1/espresso/build.
espresso-rd.verified: espresso-rd — PASSED 2026-06-24 via http-mcp prod:adminltqa, buildId 22983015, device [Galaxy S23-13], 4 sessions, 1m 13s, seen Passed on appautomation.lambdatest.com (secret stayed below the boundary; auth:{profile} -> basic_header)

espresso-vd._doc: virtual-device cloud (separate api-doc tree: App Automation API VIRTUAL Devices). VD honors different/extra caps and a different device catalog (emulators); same /v1/espresso/build shape but VD device names. TODO: capture the VD doc tree + RUN to verify the bifurcation.

xcuitest._doc: native iOS XCUITest suite — exact sibling of espresso (public api-doc: 'Execute XCUI test on the LambdaTest platform (Server 3)'). Same host correction (mobile-api).
xcuitest.prereq_upload: POST framework_upload (multipart) appFile=<.ipa/.zip>, type=xcuit-ios, name=<label> -> lt://APP...

xcui-rd._doc: real iOS devices; build host mobile-api/framework/v1/xcui/build.

doc_capture.source: https://www.testmuai.com/support/api-doc/ (public prod, no auth to read)
doc_capture.method: driven over the CHANNEL (bidi_command/script.evaluate via http-mcp) 2026-06-24 — the api-doc is a client-routed SPA whose sidebar is a collapsed accordion: NOT in sitemap.xml (only 5 selenium pages are). Method that works: click button[aria-label^='Toggle ... section'] to expand a group -> the group renders its leaves as <a href> -> read the hrefs; then browsingContext.navigate directly to a leaf href (Cloudflare managed-challenge clears itself in the live Firefox) and read the <pre>/<code> curl blocks. Synthetic .click() on a leaf is flaky; direct navigate is reliable.

devices_list: fetch-available-devices-for-running-tests-server-2 — live source for the device/mobile_version x_axes

doc_capture.cypress_automation: result-fetch only (GET): zipped-report, command-logs, console-log, network-log, enhanced-mochawesome — see frameworks.cypress
doc_capture.api_set_switcher: the api-doc splits into exactly 3 API sets (verified 2026-06-24): 'Selenium Automation API', 'Automated Screenshots API', 'App Automation API (Real Devices)'. There is NO separate 'Virtual Devices' app-automation API doc. So RD/VD for app-automation is NOT two API trees: RD is the documented framework/appium path; VD (emulators/simulators) is the SAME wire with the virtual device pool and, for appium, appium:isRealMobile:false. Espresso/XCUI framework builds are documented for real devices only.
doc_capture.host_500_finding: manual-api.lambdatest.com 500s (nginx/cfOrigin) on the LIVE routes tested with a valid Basic credential — /framework/v1/espresso/build AND /list both 500. manual-api works only for the multipart UPLOAD (app/uploadFramework). The live functional host for builds (and likely the device list) is mobile-api.lambdatest.com. Public api-doc curls show manual-api but it is stale/deprecated for these routes.

verified_curls.uploadFramework: POST manual-api.lambdatest.com/app/uploadFramework  -H 'Authorization: Basic <enc>' -H 'Content-Type: multipart/form-data' -F appFile=<file> -F type=espresso-android -F name=<label>

framework_family._doc: the native-suite (CALL) family, mapped from QA-sanctum runFramework2.py + run-verified. http-mcp stays 3 tools; this is DATA a host runner expands into http_request POSTs with auth:{profile}. One endpoint family, four types, RD/VD selected by a payload flag.
framework_family.rd_vd_switch: isVirtualDevice:true in the payload selects the VIRTUAL cloud (emulator/simulator); absent/false = real device. SAME endpoint either way — the RD/VD bifurcation is a flag, not a separate API. Upload differs: RD via manual-api/app/uploadFramework, VD via manual-api/app/upload/virtualDevice (type emulator|simulator).

payload_schema.device: ["{name}"] * parallel_builds — same device name repeated per parallel count; platformVersion is a SEPARATE key (not name-suffixed in this script, though the doc curl uses name-version)

app_inventory_prod_adminltqa._note: lt:// app references only — credentials live in the gitignored upload-response JSON / env, never here.

verified_runs.espresso-rd: PASSED buildId 22983015 — [Galaxy S23-13], 4 sessions, 1m13s, appautomation dashboard
verified_runs.xcui-vd: RUNNABLE now: ProverbialApp + ProverbialTestApp, isVirtualDevice:true, /v1/xcui/build, an iOS simulator device — pending run
verified_runs.espresso-vd: NOT runnable with current apps: androidVirtual exists but has no paired emulator test suite
verified_runs.flutter: endpoints mapped; no flutter apps uploaded for adminltqa
